limitations: Linear models only: winner is LogisticRegression, spread/total are LinearRegression. No tree-based ensembles, no feature interactions beyond what the 24 engineered features already encode.
Playoff intensity is captured by a single binary is_playoff flag — not a per-round effect. The model does not distinguish Round 1 from the Finals.
Star availability must be supplied manually via --higher-star-out / --lower-star-out; the DB has no active injury feed, so the default assumes both teams are at full strength.
Series simulation treats per-game probabilities as independent — no in-series momentum, no mid-series feature updates, no adjustment for injuries that occur during the series.
Home-court baseline is learned from home_w_pct / away_w_pct features rather than a fixed HCA offset, so it varies by matchup — review the spread before betting on raw home numbers.
Official 2026 seedings are still being finalized (play-in resolved 2026-04-17). Cross-conference home-court in the Finals uses regular-season W% as a tiebreaker.
Backtest covers only two target seasons (~166 pooled games). Error bars on every headline metric are wide; treat differences under ~5 pp as noise.
